Quality Education.reason: Course focuses on advanced accounting topics, supporting Target 4.3 ('equal access to quality tertiary education').
The course is a form of tertiary education providing professional skills for employment, directly supporting Targets 4.3 and 4.4.
evidence: 奠定相關基礎專業知識(Students will acquire and apply expertise in their respective fields.)

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Course provides professional skills for decent jobs and supports financial services, linking to Targets 8.5 and 8.10.
evidence: 奠定相關基礎專業知識
瞭解財務報導與企業經營及決策之關係

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Accounting principles are foundational for enterprises to access financial services and integrate into markets, supporting Target 9.3.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Teaching international accounting standards supports improved regulation and monitoring of global financial markets, as per Target 10.5.
evidence: 學習國際會計準則基礎的會計處理及財務報導原則

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competency includes 'sustainability issues', linking to Target 12.6, which encourages companies to integrate sustainability information into their reporting.
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Promotes transparent financial reporting via international standards, crucial for accountable institutions (Target 16.6) and reducing corruption (Target 16.5).
evidence: 學習國際會計準則基礎的會計處理及財務報導原則
理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Teaching 'international accounting standards' promotes global policy coherence for macroeconomic stability, supporting Target 17.13.
evidence: 學習國際會計準則基礎的會計處理及財務報導原則

No Poverty.reasoning: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links accounting topics to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reasoning: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links accounting topics to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reasoning: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links accounting topics to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reasoning: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) and aims to equip students with advanced professional accounting knowledge and skills, directly supporting employment and entrepreneurship (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reasoning: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links accounting topics to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reasoning: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links accounting topics to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reasoning: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links accounting topics to energy access or clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reasoning: The course provides professional accounting skills, contributing to productive employment and decent work (Target 8.5). Understanding financial reporting also supports access to financial services (Target 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reasoning: The course covers financial assets and liabilities, which are foundational accounting principles for enterprises to access financial services, supporting Target 9.3.

Reduced Inequalities.reasoning: The course teaches International Accounting Standards, which are crucial for improving the regulation and monitoring of global financial markets and institutions (Target 10.5).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reasoning: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links accounting topics to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reasoning: Core Competency B explicitly addresses 'sustainability issues,' directly linking to Target 12.6, which promotes integrating sustainability information into corporate reporting.

Climate Action.reasoning: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, the syllabus does not specifically link accounting to climate action or related targets.

Life Below Water.reasoning: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links accounting topics to marine life or ocean conservation.

Life on Land.reasoning: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links accounting topics to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reasoning: The course teaches international accounting standards and emphasizes ethics, promoting transparent financial reporting essential for accountable institutions (Target 16.6) and reducing corruption (Target 16.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reasoning: Learning international accounting standards contributes to global policy coherence and macroeconomic stability, aligning with Target 17.13.
